ioos_component: Real-time wind, wave or sea-state, and visibility observations

source: NOAA Celebrates 30 Years of Safer Marine Navigation Through PORTS®
limitations: No dollar estimate; evidence is descriptive and anecdotal; examples span multiple ports; not a causal IOOS study.

ai_extraction_notes: Drawn from webpage text on visibility sensors and transcript quotes from Tampa Bay pilots and Maryland Port Administration on fog, winds, sea state, and scheduled transit.

decision_supported: Near-coastal search-area definition and drift prediction
economic_pathway: Smaller search areas and more efficient search effort
metric: In the Block Island region, 24-hour 95th-percentile separation was 15 km using CODAR currents, 30 km using STPS, and 40-45 km using NOAA tidal currents
source: Use of Coastal Ocean Dynamics Application Radar (CODAR) Technology in U.S. Coast Guard Search and Rescue Planning
limitations: Study covers specific near-coastal regions and a 24-hour horizon; compares trajectory prediction skill rather than monetized outcomes; predates later IOOS national integration.
claim_allowed: HF radar-based surface currents outperformed NOAA tidal-current predictions for 24-hour drift prediction in the Block Island test region.
ai_extraction_notes: Metric comes from 95th-percentile separation analysis of SLDMB trajectories; not monetized but relevant to search-effort efficiency.

decision_supported: Coastal search-area definition and drift prediction
economic_pathway: Smaller search areas and more efficient search effort
metric: In the New Jersey Shelf region, 24-hour 95th-percentile separation was approximately 25 km using CODAR currents versus roughly 45 km using NOAA tidal currents
source: Use of Coastal Ocean Dynamics Application Radar (CODAR) Technology in U.S. Coast Guard Search and Rescue Planning
limitations: Study is region-specific, non-monetized, and based on historic test deployments; comparison is against NOAA tidal-current predictions available at the time.
claim_allowed: HF radar-based current fields outperformed NOAA tidal-current predictions for 24-hour drift prediction on the New Jersey Shelf in this evaluation.
ai_extraction_notes: Extracted from the report's 95th-percentile separation results for the New Jersey Shelf.

region: Mid-Atlantic Bight / U.S. coastal HF radar coverage

economic_pathway: Smaller search areas, faster localization, and more efficient use of search assets
metric: In a simulated search case using a USCG surface drifter, the HF-radar-derived search area was centered on the drifter and three times smaller than the HYCOM-derived search area; HF radar data were installed on the USCG Environmental Data Server in Spring 2009 for operational SAROPS use
source: National IOOS HF Radar Search and Rescue Project
limitations: Simulated search case, not a direct economic valuation; result compares one HF-radar-driven case to one model-driven case and may not generalize to all locations or seasons.

ai_extraction_notes: Use this as operational-effectiveness evidence that implies asset-efficiency gains rather than as a stated dollar benefit.
